rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in binary expressions by ensuring both sides are of compatible scalar types (int, real, or bool).
Resolve AGREE diagnostics about unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations by referencing only visible AADL features or locally declared eq helpers.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always match operand types in comparisons and arithmetic expressions.
Only reference identifiers that are visible in the current AADL scope or declared within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid using AADL package-qualified names in contracts.
Use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new data types or package-qualified identifiers not present in the input AADL model.
Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type annexes for output dependency contracts.
